tldr: These are complementary tools: react-native-iap handles purchases, TelemetryDeck handles privacy-first analytics — adopt TelemetryDeck alongside, not as a replacement.
why_switch: Add GDPR-friendly product analytics for subscription metrics without collecting PII
Lower analytics cost vs. heavier platforms when signal-based tracking is enough
Keep react-native-iap for actual billing while sending events to TelemetryDeck
Privacy-first signals are easier to defend in EU markets
Simpler dashboards reduce time spent on analytics maintenance
what_to_export: Existing analytics event names tied to purchase flows
Product ID list for payload tagging
User consent flow and privacy policy
Server-side purchase validation events you may want to mirror
Any current analytics destinations and their schemas
Cohort or segment definitions if you use them
export_method: react-native-iap doesn't store analytics, so there is nothing to export from the library. Inventory the events you fire on purchase callbacks today (or want to fire). If another analytics SDK is in place, document its event schema so you can mirror it as TelemetryDeck signals.
import_method: Add a TelemetryDeck client suitable for React Native — consult the TelemetryDeck official docs for the current recommended approach, as direct RN support varies. Emit signals from your react-native-iap callbacks (purchase initiated, completed, failed, restored, subscription renewed). Configure dashboards in TelemetryDeck to track conversion and retention.

concept_mapping.note: Send a signal per purchase outcome
Best emitted from server-side webhooks for accuracy
Pass as payload key to slice dashboards
Avoid PII to honor TelemetryDeck's privacy model
Build funnels in TelemetryDeck dashboards from ordered signals

gotchas: Not a replacement for react-native-iap — purchases still flow through it
TelemetryDeck is intentionally minimal; deep funnels may need another tool
Confirm React Native client support in current TelemetryDeck docs before scoping
Do not send PII; consider hashing any user identifier
Server-side webhooks give more accurate subscription lifecycle signals than client callbacks
GDPR consent flow should still gate analytics initialization

email.subject: Adding TelemetryDeck analytics alongside react-native-iap
email.body:
Hi team,

Clarifying: these aren't substitutes. react-native-iap keeps handling purchases, and we'd add TelemetryDeck for privacy-first analytics — particularly subscription metrics.

Plan: emit TelemetryDeck signals from our purchase callbacks (initiated, completed, failed, restored, renewed) and ideally from server-side webhooks for renewals. No PII. Estimating 4-12 hours for initial integration.

Flag: I need to confirm RN client support in TelemetryDeck's current docs before locking the approach. If we need deep funnels later we may revisit.

Let me know if you want me to scope further.
